_README: Gate B (tests/independence/test_provenance.py) machine-checks this manifest. One row per module-level literal constant in custom_components/addhon/client/transport/values.py. `class`: OBSERVED = Haier dictates it (any correct client sends it; identity with pyhOn is interop, not copying) | CLIENT-CHOSEN = addhOn's own identity | UNRESOLVED = still needs an independent capture (APK / mitmproxy) to be legitimately sourced. `must_differ_from_pyhon`: true for values a placeholder-inheriting copy would leave equal to pyhOn's. `pyhon_value` records pyhOn's literal (a FACT, not creative expression) purely for the anti-copy assertion. `source`: how the literal is justified -- haier-required (Haier's endpoint mandates this exact value, so it is identical across clients by necessity; NOT a claim of independent re-capture -- several coincide byte-for-byte with pyhOn precisely because Haier dictates them) | addhon-identity (addhOn's own choice) | UNRESOLVED (still needs an owner capture from the APK / mitmproxy). Human-readable companion: VALUES-PROVENANCE.md. Spec anchors point into docs/protocol/HAIER-HON-TRANSPORT.md.

constants.note: OAuth/login host. Any client hitting the hOn cloud must use it.
Custom redirect scheme (hon://mobilesdk/detect/oauth/done) the connected app is registered for.
Salesforce connected-app id the authorize endpoint validates against the redirect URI.
Implicit-flow response_type; the literal '+' is sent unencoded exactly as the server expects.
Authorize scope; spaces are sent unencoded exactly as the server expects.
AWS IoT-Data ATS endpoint for the MQTT-over-WebSocket surface.
Name of Haier's AWS IoT custom authorizer Lambda.
JSON command endpoints reject form bodies; this is the standard media type, not a pyhOn choice.
The `os` field value in the device descriptor; the server reads it verbatim.
addhOn's own client identity. pyhOn sent 'pyhOn'; inheriting that string would be a copy tell.
addhOn's fixed headless mobileId. The real app sends a per-install id; pyhOn sent 'pyhOn'.
OWNER-ACTION: confirm the current hOn app version from the APK BuildConfig.VERSION_NAME. Differs from pyhOn's stale 2.6.5, but is still an un-captured placeholder.
OWNER-ACTION: capture the real Android API level the app presents. pyhOn's 999 is a sentinel that must not be inherited; 34 is a plausible level but not yet captured.

OWNER-ACTION: capture the real device/WebView User-Agent from the hOn APK or a mitmproxy session and replace this. 'Chrome/999.999.999.999' is a synthetic sentinel no real client emits; it is currently inherited verbatim from pyhOn, so Gate B's anti-copy check for this row is wired xfail until an owner captures the real UA.
